title: How You Actually Spend Your Life

beats.narration: One number should stop you cold — 26 years of your life, gone to sleep.
beats.understand: open on a person asleep as the years fly by — 26 years asleep

beats.narration: Another thirteen years vanish at work — the same desk, day after day after day.
beats.understand: a person hunched at a desk while days blur past — 13 years at work

beats.narration: Eleven more — more than a decade — lost staring into a screen.
beats.understand: a person curled around a glowing phone as the world dims — 11 years on a screen

beats.narration: Add it all up, and almost none of your life is actually left.
beats.understand: the single data moment: the whole life as a grid, filling until only a sliver is yours

beats.narration: Only about nine years are truly yours. Nine. Spend them on something that matters.
beats.understand: a person walks out into a sunrise, arms rising — 9 years are yours
